title: An Overview of Corruption Investigations in the Philippines
gist: Multiple overlapping corruption probes — an impeachment trial, plunder charges, a Senate leadership fight, and dismissed cases involving the First Family — reflect deeply entrenched Philippine corruption ahead of the 2028 election, with substandard flood projects exposed by 2025 flooding prompting Marcos to create an Independent Commission for Infrastructure. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: Several overlapping investigations are underway in the Philippines — an impeachment trial for betrayal of public trust, plunder charges against legislators, a Senate leadership squabble, and the dismissal of cases involving the First Family — reflecting deeply entrenched bureaucratic corruption and impunity ahead of the 2028 presidential election.
insights.speaker: Mong Palatino
insights.so_what: A web of corruption probes intertwined with 2028 election positioning signals elevated political instability and governance risk in the Philippines.
insights.quote: several investigations are taking place in the Philippines reflecting the deeply entrenched corruption in the bureaucracy and the role of impunity in the unfolding political crisis ahead of the highly anticipated 2028 presidential election
insights.locator: 'An impeachment trial for betrayal of public trust'

insights.claim: Massive flooding in July 2025 exposed the proliferation of substandard and even non-existent flood-control projects, prompting President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to establish an Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) to identify perpetrators and anomalous contracts and recommend legal cases.
insights.speaker: Mong Palatino
insights.so_what: Ghost and substandard infrastructure contracts point to systemic public-works graft — a fiscal-leakage and procurement-integrity risk for Philippine infrastructure spending.
insights.quote: Massive flooding in July last year exposed the proliferation of substandard and even non-existent flood projects.
